content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.2 WARN — SettingsView.swift:11 shows user-facing label "Experimental: may be unstable and lose sort order" for the AI Sort toggle, which is user-visible copy implying an unfinished feature.
Pierre: Apple reviewers may interpret "Experimental" combined with "may be unstable and lose sort order" as evidence the shipped feature isn't finished or reliable, which risks a 2.1/2.2 completeness question even though it's not literally "beta." Metadata and release notes themselves are clean, but this in-app string is what a reviewer will actually see when toggling the feature. Consider rewording to something like "AI Sort (Beta feature not applicable) — New: sorts your inbox automatically" without "experimental/unstable" language, or gate it behind a stable disclaimer that doesn't suggest data loss risk.
